sourceShortSummaries: The party crawls through the root tunnel, loses contact with Holda, and emerges into the Feywild at twilight.
The party follows the road toward the lit manor and finds mutilated human bodies along the way.
The party steadies itself, asks for omens, and scouts the manor before approaching the gate together.
At the gate, doglike road guards question the party and accept dream-stories as the price of entry.
Inside the grounds, the party experiences the repeating end of the celebration and begins cautious social approaches.
Txarro, Kaito, and Edric question two drunken halflings who appear to be centuries out of time.
Ayveen, Edric, and Tarek trade keys to a Rakshasa for the story of Lord Revel becoming Lord Endless Ending.
The party connects Holda's mission to the naming bargain before Lord Endless Ending sends another human away and beckons Edric.
sourceLongSummaries: After a recap of Holda's contract and the door beneath Dandelion House, the party opens in the narrow root tunnel after Holda has closed the way behind them. Ayveen tries to message Holda to reopen the door and gets no response, Kaito and the others debate what they can still trust, and the tunnel changes from wood to coral- or bone-like material. Tarek compares it to a piece he picked up, the group reaches a mirrored chamber and climbs out through a ruined root house, realizing they have crossed into an otherworldly twilight landscape with distant estates and a road ahead.

Leaving the ruined root house behind, the party sees a lit celebration at a manor a few miles down the only visible road through a strange countryside of hedgerows, distant lights, and unfamiliar stars. As they debate Holda's honesty and continue toward the manor, Txarro spots a dead human with green-feathered arrows in the back and a head smashed by a maul-like force after death. The group examines tracks, pockets, and spell components, then continues past a burned pyre with human bones and another stripped corpse, recognizing that the road is dangerous but still their clearest path forward.

The bodies sour the party's mood, and Kaito turns unusually earnest in asking Edric for Night Queen prayers and the Wanderer's help finding a path out. As they approach the manor along a high hedgerow, Txarro casts Augury on whether to approach, introduce themselves, and ask the Lord about the treaty; the answer is both weal and woe. The group considers sending Tarek under invisibility, but Txarro instead summons an eastern screech owl to scout the grounds, spotting the silver gate, casual guards, guests, musicians, rowboat departures, and a tall masked figure at the house who sees the owl before stepping back inside.

The party reaches the silver gate as the doglike road guards notice them and greet them in heavily accented Common as mortals. Through questioning, the guards say they belong to the road rather than the party, that the bodies came from farther up the road, that the Twilight Kingdom has a dozen dozen houses, and that they keep the road safe from travelers carrying a mysterious bad smell. They explain that the Lord told them to let in any mortal who wanted entrance, ask whether the party brought gifts, and settle for stories of dreams from Txarro, Edric, and Kaito before gesturing the party through the gate.

After stepping through the gate, the party sees a sloping lawn, open doors, dimming lanterns, empty glasses, rowboat farewells, and musicians caught in the feeling of a party that is ending but never quite over. Edric tests the boundary by walking back out to the road and returning, confirming the guards do not stop people from leaving. The group agrees to keep one another in sight while splitting into small approaches: Txarro heads toward two halflings, while Kaito and Ayveen approach the tired string musicians, who politely refuse Kaito's offer to relieve them because the Lord was clear and they still have one more piece to play.

Txarro approaches two half-drunk halflings arguing cheerfully on the lawn, and they are amazed to meet a lizardfolk from Taelgar. They say they are from near Chardon, talk as if Chardon is still on the edge of an empire, misunderstand references to Sembara, Tollen, and the Great War, and insist they arrived that morning by giant rabbit and liberated boat. Kaito joins, uses a flask to coax names and more details, and the halflings reveal that the Lord of the Manor gives brain teasers, is serious and sad, respects or fears his neighbors, and should not be hugged; Txarro concludes they may have been at the party for hundreds of years and leads them toward wine while looking for another mortal.

Inside the manor, Ayveen and Edric find a sleeping human and then a sober tiger-headed guest on the back porch who invites them to sit and trade stories. The Rakshasa first asks for a month and a day in Ayveen's home, then accepts three of Tarek's museum keys instead. In return, he explains that he came to the Twilight Kingdom looking for lost things and pieced together the party's story: the neighbors wanted Lord Revel to send mortal guests to their house, failed with gifts and stories, and finally had a wizard challenge him to a naming contest, renaming the estate as a party ending and transforming Lord Revel Beginning into Lord Endless Ending.

Ayveen asks the Rakshasa whether he needs to leave before the grandfather clock chimes again, and he says he has time because other mortals remain at the party. After the party explains that Holda sent them, the Rakshasa advises that shouting does not work and hears Ayveen's theory that the wizard's naming created the truce or bargain Holda wants broken. He confirms it is the only bargain he knows of and suggests Lord Endless Ending may be receptive to ending it; Edric reads the Rakshasa as truthful but self-interested rather than friendly. When Edric searches for another human, the clock strikes zero, Lord Endless Ending whispers to a human who hurries out, then locks eyes with Edric and gestures him closer as Txarro sends his owl after the departing human.

sourceEntries.title: Through The Root
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The session opens with setup and recap, then follows the party through the bone-like root tunnel, out into the Feywild, and through their first reactions and character-state descriptions; the next beat begins when they leave the ruined house and start down the road.

Ayveen tries to message Holda to reopen the door and gets no response, Kaito and the others debate what they can still trust, and the tunnel changes from wood to coral- or bone-like material. Tarek compares it to a piece he picked up, the group reaches a mirrored chamber and climbs out through a ruined root house, realizing they have crossed into an otherworldly twilight landscape with distant estates and a road ahead.

locationHints.context: root tunnel from the subcellar to the ruined root house on the far side
locationHints.notes: The party moves from the tunnel beneath Dandelion House into the Feywild region later named the Twilight Kingdom.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.notes: He hired the party, closed the door behind them, and does not respond to Ayveen's message.

npcDetails.name: Alden
npcDetails.notes: The recap confirms Kaito previously detected thoughts on Alden but not on Holda.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.context: master of the manor beyond the door
npcDetails.notes: Holda described him only as the man or master of the house; his identity is clarified later in the session.

npcDetails.name: unnamed elven archivist
npcDetails.notes: The recap repeats that an elf calling himself an archivist gave Holda the deed to Dandelion House.

itemDetails.name: ancient Sembaran contract
itemDetails.notes: Tarek's earlier concern is restated: the 400-year-old contract was written in contemporary Sembaran.

itemDetails.name: glowing gold key
itemDetails.notes: Alden used it in the recap to open the lower locked door beneath Dandelion House.

itemDetails.name: bone-like root material
itemDetails.notes: The tunnel and far chamber become coral- or bone-like, and Tarek compares the material to a loose piece he found.

organizationDetails.name: Drankorian Empire
organizationDetails.notes: Dandelion House is recalled as a late Drankorian architectural work.

sourceEntries.title: Well Met, Mortals
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party reaches the open gate, glimpses the masked lord through the owl, questions the doglike road guards about the bodies, the Twilight Kingdom, and the party, then pays entry with stories of dreams; the next beat begins when the guards let them through into the grounds.

locationHints.context: silver gate to Lord Endless Ending's manor
locationHints.notes: The party is still outside the manor grounds until the guards admit them at the end of the beat.

npcDetails.name: road guards
npcDetails.notes: They sniff for the bad smell, answer questions about the road and Twilight Kingdom, and trade entry for dreams.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: The guards say he instructed them to admit any mortal who wanted entrance.

npcDetails.name: unnamed mortal guests
npcDetails.notes: The guards mention halflings, a dwarf, and other mortals who have come and gone.

sourceEntries.title: The Ending Party
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party enters the grounds, feels the looped not-quite-frozen ending of the celebration, tests that they can leave, and splits into small social approaches while Kaito tries to engage the exhausted musicians; the next beat starts with Txarro and others speaking to the halflings.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Inside the grounds, the party experiences the repeating end of the celebration and begins cautious social approaches.
sourceEntries.longSummary: After stepping through the gate, the party sees a sloping lawn, open doors, dimming lanterns, empty glasses, rowboat farewells, and musicians caught in the feeling of a party that is ending but never quite over. locationHints.context: grounds, lawns, patios, and gate area during the ending party
locationHints.notes: The scene establishes the party's looping, unresolved ending.

npcDetails.name: road guards
npcDetails.notes: They confirm Edric can leave and re-enter freely.

npcDetails.name: string musicians
npcDetails.notes: They play beautifully but are exhausted and bound to perform one more piece.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: The musicians cite his clear instruction as the reason Kaito cannot take over.

itemDetails.name: unusual string instruments
itemDetails.notes: The trio plays cello-, violin-, and viola-like instruments with more strings than expected.

sourceEntries.title: The Old Halflings
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Txarro, Kaito, and others question two drunken halflings, discover they are wildly out of time, learn they came from Chardon by rabbit and boat, and get their impressions of the Lord of the Manor before trying to lead them toward wine and other mortals; the next beat shifts to Ayveen and Edric finding the tiger-like guest.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Txarro, Kaito, and Edric question two drunken halflings who appear to be centuries out of time.

locationHints.context: party lawn and route back toward the bar
locationHints.notes: The conversation starts near the lawn and ends with the halflings being guided toward wine.

npcDetails.name: Pandel
npcDetails.notes: His name is uncertainly rendered in the transcript but Pandel is the clearest spoken form.

npcDetails.name: unnamed halfling companion
npcDetails.notes: The second name is not reliably established in the transcript.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: The halflings describe him as serious, sad, fond of brain teasers, and respectful or afraid of his neighbors.

npcDetails.name: Rakshasa
npcDetails.notes: The halflings call him a tiger but say he is not actually a tiger.

itemDetails.name: Kaito's flask
itemDetails.notes: Kaito uses it to offer the halflings a small drink and keep the conversation moving.

itemDetails.name: liberated boat
itemDetails.notes: The halflings claim they came to the party by a boat they liberated after riding a giant rabbit.

sourceEntries.title: The Tiger's Story
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Ayveen and Edric find the sober tiger-like guest, negotiate for his knowledge with Tarek's keys, and hear his account of Lord Revel, the naming contest, and the transformation into Lord Endless Ending; the next beat begins when Ayveen turns the story toward the immediate question of leaving and the truce.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Ayveen, Edric, and Tarek trade keys to a Rakshasa for the story of Lord Revel becoming Lord Endless Ending.

locationHints.notes: The beat follows Ayveen and Edric from the bar area to the Rakshasa's porch conversation.

npcDetails.name: Rakshasa
npcDetails.notes: He trades information for three of Tarek's keys and identifies himself indirectly through later table discussion.

npcDetails.name: Lord Revel
npcDetails.context: former name of Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: The Rakshasa says Lord Revel Beginning was transformed by the naming contest.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.context: current host of the ending party
npcDetails.notes: The Rakshasa's story explains how Revel became Endless Ending.

npcDetails.name: unnamed wizard neighbor
npcDetails.notes: The wizard named the estate as a party ending and imposed the bargain.

npcDetails.name: unnamed sleeping human
npcDetails.context: mortal guest sleeping behind a couch
npcDetails.notes: Ayveen and Edric notice this human while looking for wine and other mortals.

itemDetails.name: Tarek's museum keys
itemDetails.notes: The Rakshasa selects three keys as payment for his story.

itemDetails.name: grandfather clock
itemDetails.notes: The Rakshasa says the clock chimes from time to time before Lord Endless Ending sends another mortal down the road.

sourceEntries.title: The Truce Theory
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The group connects the naming contest to Holda's mission, considers whether the Lord is bound by a truce he cannot break himself, learns another human may be moving through the house, and ends as Lord Endless Ending sends that human away and beckons Edric closer; the session closes on the party's working theory.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party connects Holda's mission to the naming bargain before Lord Endless Ending sends another human away and beckons Edric.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Ayveen asks the Rakshasa whether he needs to leave before the grandfather clock chimes again, and he says he has time because other mortals remain at the party. After the party explains that Holda sent them, the Rakshasa advises that shouting does not work and hears Ayveen's theory that the wizard's naming created the truce or bargain Holda wants broken. He confirms it is the only bargain he knows of and suggests Lord Endless Ending may be receptive to ending it; Edric reads the Rakshasa as truthful but self-interested rather than friendly. When Edric searches for another human, the clock strikes zero, Lord Endless Ending whispers to a human who hurries out, then locks eyes with Edric and gestures him closer as Txarro sends his owl after the departing human.

locationHints.context: back porch, bar area, and ballroom
locationHints.notes: The beat begins with the Rakshasa conversation and ends across the ballroom as Lord Endless Ending appears.

npcDetails.name: Rakshasa
npcDetails.notes: He gives advice, comments on the bargain, and is judged truthful but self-interested.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: He whispers to a human after the clock strikes zero and then beckons Edric.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.context: angry man who sent the party
npcDetails.notes: The Rakshasa saw Holda's failed conversation with the Lord and says bullying a lord in his own manor was unlikely to work.

npcDetails.name: unnamed human
npcDetails.context: mortal guest sent out after the clock strikes zero
npcDetails.notes: Lord Endless Ending whispers to this human, who then hurries out the door.

npcDetails.name: road guards
npcDetails.context: keepers of the roads between houses
npcDetails.notes: The Rakshasa says they guard what travels the roads, not the party guests.

npcDetails.name: Pandel and companion
npcDetails.notes: They do not make it past the bar and can be heard clattering among the bottles.

itemDetails.name: grandfather clock
itemDetails.notes: The clock strikes zero just before Lord Endless Ending sends the human away.

itemDetails.name: naming bargain
itemDetails.notes: The party treats the wizard's naming contest bargain as the likely treaty or truce Holda wants broken.

sourceEntries.title: Well Met, Mortals
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party reaches the open gate, glimpses the masked lord through the owl, questions the doglike road guards about the bodies, the Twilight Kingdom, and the party, then pays entry with stories of dreams; the next beat begins when the guards let them through into the grounds.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: At the gate, doglike road guards question the party and accept dream-stories as the price of entry.
sourceEntries.longSummary: The party reaches the silver gate as the doglike road guards notice them and greet them in heavily accented Common as mortals. Through questioning, the guards say they belong to the road rather than the party, that the bodies came from farther up the road, that the Twilight Kingdom has a dozen dozen houses, and that they keep the road safe from travelers carrying a mysterious bad smell. They explain that the Lord told them to let in any mortal who wanted entrance, ask whether the party brought gifts, and settle for stories of dreams from Txarro, Edric, and Kaito before gesturing the party through the gate.

locationHints.context: silver gate to Lord Endless Ending's manor
locationHints.notes: The party is still outside the manor grounds until the guards admit them at the end of the beat.

npcDetails.name: road guards
npcDetails.notes: They sniff for the bad smell, answer questions about the road and Twilight Kingdom, and trade entry for dreams.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: The guards say he instructed them to admit any mortal who wanted entrance.

npcDetails.name: unnamed mortal guests
npcDetails.notes: The guards mention halflings, a dwarf, and other mortals who have come and gone.

recapBlocks.title: The Ending Party
sourceShortSummaries: Inside the grounds, the party experiences the repeating end of the celebration and begins cautious social approaches.
sourceLongSummaries: After stepping through the gate, the party sees a sloping lawn, open doors, dimming lanterns, empty glasses, rowboat farewells, and musicians caught in the feeling of a party that is ending but never quite over. Edric tests the boundary by walking back out to the road and returning, confirming the guards do not stop people from leaving. The group agrees to keep one another in sight while splitting into small approaches: Txarro heads toward two halflings, while Kaito and Ayveen approach the tired string musicians, who politely refuse Kaito's offer to relieve them because the Lord was clear and they still have one more piece to play.

sourceEntries.title: The Ending Party
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The party enters the grounds, feels the looped not-quite-frozen ending of the celebration, tests that they can leave, and splits into small social approaches while Kaito tries to engage the exhausted musicians; the next beat starts with Txarro and others speaking to the halflings.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Inside the grounds, the party experiences the repeating end of the celebration and begins cautious social approaches.
sourceEntries.longSummary: After stepping through the gate, the party sees a sloping lawn, open doors, dimming lanterns, empty glasses, rowboat farewells, and musicians caught in the feeling of a party that is ending but never quite over. Edric tests the boundary by walking back out to the road and returning, confirming the guards do not stop people from leaving. The group agrees to keep one another in sight while splitting into small approaches: Txarro heads toward two halflings, while Kaito and Ayveen approach the tired string musicians, who politely refuse Kaito's offer to relieve them because the Lord was clear and they still have one more piece to play.

locationHints.context: grounds, lawns, patios, and gate area during the ending party
locationHints.notes: The scene establishes the party's looping, unresolved ending.

npcDetails.name: road guards
npcDetails.notes: They confirm Edric can leave and re-enter freely.

npcDetails.name: string musicians
npcDetails.notes: They play beautifully but are exhausted and bound to perform one more piece.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: The musicians cite his clear instruction as the reason Kaito cannot take over.

itemDetails.name: unusual string instruments
itemDetails.notes: The trio plays cello-, violin-, and viola-like instruments with more strings than expected.

recapBlocks.title: The Old Halflings
sourceShortSummaries: Txarro, Kaito, and Edric question two drunken halflings who appear to be centuries out of time.
sourceLongSummaries: Txarro approaches two half-drunk halflings arguing cheerfully on the lawn, and they are amazed to meet a lizardfolk from Taelgar. They say they are from near Chardon, talk as if Chardon is still on the edge of an empire, misunderstand references to Sembara, Tollen, and the Great War, and insist they arrived that morning by giant rabbit and liberated boat. Kaito joins, uses a flask to coax names and more details, and the halflings reveal that the Lord of the Manor gives brain teasers, is serious and sad, respects or fears his neighbors, and should not be hugged; Txarro concludes they may have been at the party for hundreds of years and leads them toward wine while looking for another mortal.

sourceEntries.title: The Old Halflings
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Txarro, Kaito, and others question two drunken halflings, discover they are wildly out of time, learn they came from Chardon by rabbit and boat, and get their impressions of the Lord of the Manor before trying to lead them toward wine and other mortals; the next beat shifts to Ayveen and Edric finding the tiger-like guest.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Txarro, Kaito, and Edric question two drunken halflings who appear to be centuries out of time.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Txarro approaches two half-drunk halflings arguing cheerfully on the lawn, and they are amazed to meet a lizardfolk from Taelgar. They say they are from near Chardon, talk as if Chardon is still on the edge of an empire, misunderstand references to Sembara, Tollen, and the Great War, and insist they arrived that morning by giant rabbit and liberated boat. Kaito joins, uses a flask to coax names and more details, and the halflings reveal that the Lord of the Manor gives brain teasers, is serious and sad, respects or fears his neighbors, and should not be hugged; Txarro concludes they may have been at the party for hundreds of years and leads them toward wine while looking for another mortal.

locationHints.context: party lawn and route back toward the bar
locationHints.notes: The conversation starts near the lawn and ends with the halflings being guided toward wine.

npcDetails.name: Pandel
npcDetails.notes: His name is uncertainly rendered in the transcript but Pandel is the clearest spoken form.

npcDetails.name: unnamed halfling companion
npcDetails.notes: The second name is not reliably established in the transcript.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: The halflings describe him as serious, sad, fond of brain teasers, and respectful or afraid of his neighbors.

npcDetails.name: Rakshasa
npcDetails.notes: The halflings call him a tiger but say he is not actually a tiger.

itemDetails.name: Kaito's flask
itemDetails.notes: Kaito uses it to offer the halflings a small drink and keep the conversation moving.

itemDetails.name: liberated boat
itemDetails.notes: The halflings claim they came to the party by a boat they liberated after riding a giant rabbit.

recapBlocks.title: The Tiger's Story
sourceShortSummaries: Ayveen, Edric, and Tarek trade keys to a Rakshasa for the story of Lord Revel becoming Lord Endless Ending.
sourceLongSummaries: Inside the manor, Ayveen and Edric find a sleeping human and then a sober tiger-headed guest on the back porch who invites them to sit and trade stories. The Rakshasa first asks for a month and a day in Ayveen's home, then accepts three of Tarek's museum keys instead. In return, he explains that he came to the Twilight Kingdom looking for lost things and pieced together the party's story: the neighbors wanted Lord Revel to send mortal guests to their house, failed with gifts and stories, and finally had a wizard challenge him to a naming contest, renaming the estate as a party ending and transforming Lord Revel Beginning into Lord Endless Ending.

sourceEntries.title: The Tiger's Story
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: Ayveen and Edric find the sober tiger-like guest, negotiate for his knowledge with Tarek's keys, and hear his account of Lord Revel, the naming contest, and the transformation into Lord Endless Ending; the next beat begins when Ayveen turns the story toward the immediate question of leaving and the truce.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: Ayveen, Edric, and Tarek trade keys to a Rakshasa for the story of Lord Revel becoming Lord Endless Ending.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Inside the manor, Ayveen and Edric find a sleeping human and then a sober tiger-headed guest on the back porch who invites them to sit and trade stories. The Rakshasa first asks for a month and a day in Ayveen's home, then accepts three of Tarek's museum keys instead. In return, he explains that he came to the Twilight Kingdom looking for lost things and pieced together the party's story: the neighbors wanted Lord Revel to send mortal guests to their house, failed with gifts and stories, and finally had a wizard challenge him to a naming contest, renaming the estate as a party ending and transforming Lord Revel Beginning into Lord Endless Ending.

locationHints.notes: The beat follows Ayveen and Edric from the bar area to the Rakshasa's porch conversation.

npcDetails.name: Rakshasa
npcDetails.notes: He trades information for three of Tarek's keys and identifies himself indirectly through later table discussion.

npcDetails.name: Lord Revel
npcDetails.context: former name of Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: The Rakshasa says Lord Revel Beginning was transformed by the naming contest.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.context: current host of the ending party
npcDetails.notes: The Rakshasa's story explains how Revel became Endless Ending.

npcDetails.name: unnamed wizard neighbor
npcDetails.notes: The wizard named the estate as a party ending and imposed the bargain.

npcDetails.name: unnamed sleeping human
npcDetails.context: mortal guest sleeping behind a couch
npcDetails.notes: Ayveen and Edric notice this human while looking for wine and other mortals.

itemDetails.name: Tarek's museum keys
itemDetails.notes: The Rakshasa selects three keys as payment for his story.

itemDetails.name: grandfather clock
itemDetails.notes: The Rakshasa says the clock chimes from time to time before Lord Endless Ending sends another mortal down the road.

recapBlocks.title: The Truce Theory
sourceShortSummaries: The party connects Holda's mission to the naming bargain before Lord Endless Ending sends another human away and beckons Edric.
sourceLongSummaries: Ayveen asks the Rakshasa whether he needs to leave before the grandfather clock chimes again, and he says he has time because other mortals remain at the party. After the party explains that Holda sent them, the Rakshasa advises that shouting does not work and hears Ayveen's theory that the wizard's naming created the truce or bargain Holda wants broken. He confirms it is the only bargain he knows of and suggests Lord Endless Ending may be receptive to ending it; Edric reads the Rakshasa as truthful but self-interested rather than friendly. When Edric searches for another human, the clock strikes zero, Lord Endless Ending whispers to a human who hurries out, then locks eyes with Edric and gestures him closer as Txarro sends his owl after the departing human.

sourceEntries.title: The Truce Theory
sourceEntries.boundaryReason: The group connects the naming contest to Holda's mission, considers whether the Lord is bound by a truce he cannot break himself, learns another human may be moving through the house, and ends as Lord Endless Ending sends that human away and beckons Edric closer; the session closes on the party's working theory.
sourceEntries.shortSummary: The party connects Holda's mission to the naming bargain before Lord Endless Ending sends another human away and beckons Edric.
sourceEntries.longSummary: Ayveen asks the Rakshasa whether he needs to leave before the grandfather clock chimes again, and he says he has time because other mortals remain at the party. After the party explains that Holda sent them, the Rakshasa advises that shouting does not work and hears Ayveen's theory that the wizard's naming created the truce or bargain Holda wants broken. He confirms it is the only bargain he knows of and suggests Lord Endless Ending may be receptive to ending it; Edric reads the Rakshasa as truthful but self-interested rather than friendly. When Edric searches for another human, the clock strikes zero, Lord Endless Ending whispers to a human who hurries out, then locks eyes with Edric and gestures him closer as Txarro sends his owl after the departing human.

locationHints.context: back porch, bar area, and ballroom
locationHints.notes: The beat begins with the Rakshasa conversation and ends across the ballroom as Lord Endless Ending appears.

npcDetails.name: Rakshasa
npcDetails.notes: He gives advice, comments on the bargain, and is judged truthful but self-interested.

npcDetails.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcDetails.notes: He whispers to a human after the clock strikes zero and then beckons Edric.

npcDetails.name: Lord Holda
npcDetails.context: angry man who sent the party
npcDetails.notes: The Rakshasa saw Holda's failed conversation with the Lord and says bullying a lord in his own manor was unlikely to work.

npcDetails.name: unnamed human
npcDetails.context: mortal guest sent out after the clock strikes zero
npcDetails.notes: Lord Endless Ending whispers to this human, who then hurries out the door.

npcDetails.name: road guards
npcDetails.context: keepers of the roads between houses
npcDetails.notes: The Rakshasa says they guard what travels the roads, not the party guests.

npcDetails.name: Pandel and companion
npcDetails.notes: They do not make it past the bar and can be heard clattering among the bottles.

itemDetails.name: grandfather clock
itemDetails.notes: The clock strikes zero just before Lord Endless Ending sends the human away.

itemDetails.name: naming bargain
itemDetails.notes: The party treats the wizard's naming contest bargain as the likely treaty or truce Holda wants broken.
